name: Mexican Summer Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and soak the beans overnight. The next day, cook them on low in a slow cooker while you are at work, then drain and rinse before adding to the pot.
2. In a medium saucepan, sauté onion and garlic in water until tender.
3. Add fresh green beans and banana peppers, continuing to sauté for 3 minutes.
4. Keep an eye on the water level so it doesn't dry out completely.
5. Just before the water dries up entirely, add the zucchini and continue sautéing for 2 more minutes. If needed, add a little extra water to prevent scorching the vegetables.
6. Add fresh tomatoes, beans, wine, and spices. Cook until the mixture thickens and forms a nice sauce.
7. For those on a restricted salt diet, no additional salt is required, though some may choose to add sweetener for flavor. The dish is quite good even without salt, and it tastes delicious.
